bib.title: Composing Specications for Coordination
bib.citation: International Conference on Coordination Languages and Models, 118-133, 1999
bib.author: Carlo Montangero and Laura Semini
bib.abstract: We introduce Oikos_adtl, a specification language for distributed systems based on asynchronous communication via remot writings. The language is designed to support the composition of specifications. It allows expressing the global properties of a system in terms of the local properties of the components of coordination templates. Oikos-adtl is based on an asynchronous, distributed, temporal logic, which extends Unity to deal with components and events.We present the specification language and its semantics, introduce a number of compositionality theorems, and discuss some coordination templates. A fragment of a standard case study is used to validate pratmatically the approach, with respect to expressiveness and work-ability.
